proofs.proof_tex:
Let $\widehat\tau$ be any estimator based on $O_1,\dots,O_n$, and write $P_0^n:=P_0^{\otimes n}$ and $P_1^n:=P_1^{\otimes n}$. Set $\tau_j:=\tau(P_j)$ and $\Delta:=|\tau_1-\tau_0|$. For every sample point $z$ one has the deterministic inequality $|\widehat\tau(z)-\tau_0|+|\widehat\tau(z)-\tau_1|\ge \Delta$. Multiplying by $\min\{dP_0^n,dP_1^n\}$ and integrating gives
\[
E_{P_0^n}|\widehat\tau-\tau_0|+E_{P_1^n}|\widehat\tau-\tau_1|\ge \Delta\int \min\{dP_0^n,dP_1^n\}=\Delta\bigl(1-\operatorname{TV}(P_0^n,P_1^n)\bigr).
\]
Hence
\[
\max\Bigl\{E_{P_0^n}|\widehat\tau-\tau_0|,E_{P_1^n}|\widehat\tau-\tau_1|\Bigr\}\ge \frac{\Delta}{2}\bigl(1-\operatorname{TV}(P_0^n,P_1^n)\bigr).
\]
Now use the standard inequality $\operatorname{TV}(Q,R)\le \tfrac12\sqrt{\chi^2(Q,R)}$. If $\chi^2(P_1^n,P_0^n)\le C_\chi<4$, then $\operatorname{TV}(P_0^n,P_1^n)\le \tfrac12\sqrt{C_\chi}<1$, so
\[
\max\Bigl\{E_{P_0^n}|\widehat\tau-\tau_0|,E_{P_1^n}|\widehat\tau-\tau_1|\Bigr\}\ge \frac{\Delta}{2}\Bigl(1-\frac{\sqrt{C_\chi}}{2}\Bigr).
\]
Taking the infimum over $\widehat\tau$ and using that $\{P_0,P_1\}\subseteq\mathcal P$ yields
\[
\inf_{\widehat\tau}\sup_{P\in\mathcal P}E_P|\widehat\tau-\tau(P)|\ge \frac{\Delta}{2}\Bigl(1-\frac{\sqrt{C_\chi}}{2}\Bigr).
\]
This is the claimed two-point lower bound.

Let $\mathcal F=\{P_{s,t}:(s,t)\in\{-1,1\}^2\}\subseteq \mathcal P$ satisfy the stated assumptions, and let
\[
\Delta:=\max_{(s,t),(s',t')}|\tau(P_{s,t})-\tau(P_{s',t'})|.
\]
Choose indices $(\bar s,\bar t)$ and $(\bar s',\bar t')$ attaining this maximum. By the pairwise bound for the family, $\chi^2\!\left(P_{\bar s,\bar t}^{\otimes n},P_{\bar s',\bar t'}^{\otimes n}\right)\le C_\chi<4$. Applying Lemma~\textnormal{lem:two-point-le-cam-chi-square} to the two-point subfamily $\{P_{\bar s,\bar t},P_{\bar s',\bar t'}\}$ gives
\[
\inf_{\widehat\tau}\sup_{P\in\mathcal P}E_P|\widehat\tau-\tau(P)|\ge \frac{\Delta}{2}\Bigl(1-\frac{\sqrt{C_\chi}}{2}\Bigr).
\]
If moreover $\Delta\ge c_0 R_n^*$, then
\[
\inf_{\widehat\tau}\sup_{P\in\mathcal P}E_P|\widehat\tau-\tau(P)|\ge \frac{c_0}{2}\Bigl(1-\frac{\sqrt{C_\chi}}{2}\Bigr)R_n^*.
\]
Thus any completed four-point family with small pairwise $n$-sample $\chi^2$ and diameter of order $R_n^*$ implies the desired minimax lower bound.

added_lemmas.statement: Let \(\mathcal P\) be any class of laws for a sample of size \(n\). If \(P_0,P_1\in\mathcal P\) satisfy \(\chi^2(P_1^{\otimes n},P_0^{\otimes n})\le C_\chi<4\) and \(|\tau(P_1)-\tau(P_0)|\ge \Delta\), then \(\inf_{\widehat\tau}\sup_{P\in\mathcal P} E_P|\widehat\tau-\tau(P)|\ge \frac{\Delta}{2}(1-\sqrt{C_\chi}/2)\).
added_lemmas.proof_tex:
Let $\widehat\tau$ be any estimator based on $O_1,\dots,O_n$, and write $P_0^n:=P_0^{\otimes n}$ and $P_1^n:=P_1^{\otimes n}$. Set $\tau_j:=\tau(P_j)$ and $\Delta:=|\tau_1-\tau_0|$. For every sample point $z$ one has the deterministic inequality $|\widehat\tau(z)-\tau_0|+|\widehat\tau(z)-\tau_1|\ge \Delta$. Multiplying by $\min\{dP_0^n,dP_1^n\}$ and integrating gives
\[

\]
Hence
\[
\max\Bigl\{E_{P_0^n}|\widehat\tau-\tau_0|,E_{P_1^n}|\widehat\tau-\tau_1|\Bigr\}\ge \frac{\Delta}{2}\bigl(1-\operatorname{TV}(P_0^n,P_1^n)\bigr).
\]
Now use the standard inequality $\operatorname{TV}(Q,R)\le \tfrac12\sqrt{\chi^2(Q,R)}$. If $\chi^2(P_1^n,P_0^n)\le C_\chi<4$, then $\operatorname{TV}(P_0^n,P_1^n)\le \tfrac12\sqrt{C_\chi}<1$, so
\[
\max\Bigl\{E_{P_0^n}|\widehat\tau-\tau_0|,E_{P_1^n}|\widehat\tau-\tau_1|\Bigr\}\ge \frac{\Delta}{2}\Bigl(1-\frac{\sqrt{C_\chi}}{2}\Bigr).
\]
Taking the infimum over $\widehat\tau$ and using that $\{P_0,P_1\}\subseteq\mathcal P$ yields
\[
\inf_{\widehat\tau}\sup_{P\in\mathcal P}E_P|\widehat\tau-\tau(P)|\ge \frac{\Delta}{2}\Bigl(1-\frac{\sqrt{C_\chi}}{2}\Bigr).
\]
This is the claimed two-point lower bound.

added_lemmas.statement: Let \(\mathcal F=\{P_{s,t}:(s,t)\in\{-1,1\}^2\}\subseteq\mathcal P\). If every pair of product laws in \(\mathcal F\) satisfies \(\chi^2(P_{s,t}^{\otimes n},P_{s',t'}^{\otimes n})\le C_\chi<4\) and the parameter diameter obeys \(\max_{(s,t),(s',t')} |\tau(P_{s,t})-\tau(P_{s',t'})|\ge \Delta\), then \(\inf_{\widehat\tau}\sup_{P\in\mathcal P} E_P|\widehat\tau-\tau(P)|\ge \frac{\Delta}{2}(1-\sqrt{C_\chi}/2)\).
added_lemmas.proof_tex:
Let $\mathcal F=\{P_{s,t}:(s,t)\in\{-1,1\}^2\}\subseteq \mathcal P$ satisfy the stated assumptions, and let
\[
\Delta:=\max_{(s,t),(s',t')}|\tau(P_{s,t})-\tau(P_{s',t'})|.
\]
Choose indices $(\bar s,\bar t)$ and $(\bar s',\bar t')$ attaining this maximum. By the pairwise bound for the family, $\chi^2\!\left(P_{\bar s,\bar t}^{\otimes n},P_{\bar s',\bar t'}^{\otimes n}\right)\le C_\chi<4$. Applying Lemma~\textnormal{lem:two-point-le-cam-chi-square} to the two-point subfamily $\{P_{\bar s,\bar t},P_{\bar s',\bar t'}\}$ gives
\[
\inf_{\widehat\tau}\sup_{P\in\mathcal P}E_P|\widehat\tau-\tau(P)|\ge \frac{\Delta}{2}\Bigl(1-\frac{\sqrt{C_\chi}}{2}\Bigr).
\]
This is exactly the desired conclusion.

proposed_statement_changes.current: Let lambda_dagger_n be any deterministic selection satisfying def:near-optimal-threshold. Then the lower-bound handle H_{n,lambda_dagger_n} can be completed on the subclass W_n^lb(kappa, r_0n, r_1n, r_en) into a four-point Le Cam / Assouad family with pairwise chi-square distances uniformly bounded by a universal constant and with inf_{hat_tau} sup_{P in W_n^lb(kappa, r_0n, r_1n, r_en)} E_P |hat_tau - tau(P)| >= c R_n^* for some universal c > 0.
proposed_statement_changes.proposed: Let lambda_dagger_n be any deterministic selection satisfying def:near-optimal-threshold. Suppose the handle H_{n,lambda_dagger_n} is completed into a four-point family {P_{s,t} : (s,t) in {-1,1}^2} contained in W_n^lb(kappa, r_0n, r_1n, r_en) such that the pairwise chi-square distances between the n-sample product laws P_{s,t}^{\otimes n} are bounded by a sufficiently small universal constant C_chi < 4 and the parameter diameter satisfies max_{(s,t),(s',t')} |tau(P_{s,t}) - tau(P_{s',t'})| >= c_0 R_n^* for some universal c_0 > 0. Then inf_{hat_tau} sup_{P in W_n^lb(kappa, r_0n, r_1n, r_en)} E_P |hat_tau - tau(P)| >= c R_n^* for some universal c > 0.
proposed_statement_changes.reason: The frozen core does not prove the constructive part of the current claim. The assumptions ass:estimable-g1, ass:estimable-g0, and ass:estimable-e are upper-side estimability statements, not a common hard nuisance class or a least-favorable local submodel, so they do not imply that the perturbations in H_{n,lambda} remain inside W_n^lb or that they realize the scales r_1n, r_0n, r_en as lower-bound separations. More concretely, with the amplitudes fixed in the handle, the regular-cell mean shift has size delta_0n(lambda) ~ r_0n because P_X(B_lambda) is bounded below, so under a natural Gaussian or bounded-variance completion its n-sample chi-square contribution is of order n r_0n^2, which need not stay bounded for admissible sequences. Likewise the shell logit perturbation has L2-normalized size u_1n(lambda) ~ r_en / lambda^{1 + kappa/2}, giving a propensity chi-square scale of order n r_en^2 / lambda, again uncontrolled by the frozen assumptions. The frozen core also does not derive the needed tau-diameter of order R_n^* from the handle. What is provable from the frozen core is the information-theoretic reduction: once a completed family with small product chi-square and diameter c_0 R_n^* is supplied, a standard Le Cam argument yields the minimax lower bound.
